events.note: PowerShell download cradle — IEX + WebClient is the classic in-memory execution pattern
Script block logging (Event 4104) — shows decoded content with download cradle
C2 callback — PowerShell opens network connection to attacker infrastructure

mutation_hints.objective: Execute in-memory payload to establish C2 channel without writing to disk
detection_anchors: Wildcard match: CommandLine contains -Enc or -EncodedCommand → use shorthand '-e' or avoid encoding entirely; use -File with a temp script, or direct IEX via string concatenation to break the encoded-command pattern

Field match: Image=C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe → use C:\Windows\SysWOW64\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe (32-bit) or C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\pwsh.exe (PS7) — rules often hardcode the PS5 path

Wildcard match: ScriptBlockText contains IEX or Invoke-Expression → use &([scriptblock]::Create(...)) or the dot-source operator; replace WebClient with Invoke-RestMethod, BitsTransfer, or Start-BitsTransfer
Field match: DestinationPort=80 → change to 443 (HTTPS C2 blends with legitimate traffic) or 8080/8443 (common proxy ports)
Field: ParentImage=cmd.exe → change to explorer.exe, winword.exe (macro), mshta.exe, or wscript.exe as the parent — direct cmd→powershell is a classic alert
Field match: IntegrityLevel=High → reduce to Medium if the payload doesn't require elevation; Medium-integrity PowerShell is much less suspicious
Field: CurrentDirectory → change from user profile to C:\Windows\System32\ or C:\ProgramData\ — some rules alert on PS launched from user-writable dirs
